SEARCH
Package client.app

Class Navigator

java.lang.Object
client.app.Navigator
public class Navigator extends Object
Owns the content area inside MainShell and the map of registered screens. Each screen is an FXML path plus a title/subtitle pair shown in the topbar. Calling go(String) loads the FXML, swaps it into the content area, and toggles the :active pseudo-class on the matching sidebar button.

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested Classes
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    static final record 
    Navigator.Screen
    Describes one navigable screen.

    Pseudo-class toggled on the active sidebar button.

    The pane screens are swapped into.
    private BaseController
    currentController
    The active screen's controller (for firing its onHide before a swap).

    Id of the currently displayed screen, or null if none yet.

    bindNavButton(String screenId, Button btn)
    Associates a sidebar button with a screen id and wires its click to navigate there.

    Builds a sidebar nav button styled by client.css, with the icon and label laid out side by side.

    Returns the content pane this navigator swaps screens into.
    Navigator.Screen
    currentScreen()
    Returns the currently displayed screen, or null if none yet.
    void
    go(String id)
    Navigates to the screen with the given id: hides the outgoing screen, loads and shows the new FXML, and marks the matching sidebar button active.
    private Object
    instantiate(Class<?> type)
    Constructs a controller class via reflection, picking a constructor that takes (NetworkService, Session, Navigator) if available, otherwise the (NetworkService, Session) or no-arg form.
    void
    register(Navigator.Screen s)
    Registers a screen so it can later be navigated to by its id.

  • Constructor Details

    • Navigator

      public Navigator(StackPane contentArea, NetworkService network, Session session, Consumer<Navigator.Screen> onScreenChange)
      Creates a navigator bound to the shell's content area.
      Parameters:
      contentArea - the pane whose children are swapped on navigation
      network - network service handed to controllers that need it
      session - the current client session handed to controllers
      onScreenChange - callback invoked with the new Navigator.Screen after each navigation (may be null)

  • Method Details

    • register

      public void register(Navigator.Screen s)
      Registers a screen so it can later be navigated to by its id.
      Parameters:
      s - the screen definition to register

    • bindNavButton

      public void bindNavButton(String screenId, Button btn)
      Associates a sidebar button with a screen id and wires its click to navigate there.
      Parameters:
      screenId - id of the screen the button activates
      btn - the sidebar button to bind

    • currentScreen

      public Navigator.Screen currentScreen()
      Returns the currently displayed screen, or null if none yet.
      Returns:
      the currently displayed screen, or null if none yet

    • go

      public void go(String id)
      Navigates to the screen with the given id: hides the outgoing screen, loads and shows the new FXML, and marks the matching sidebar button active.
      Parameters:
      id - id of a registered screen
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if no screen is registered under id

    • instantiate

      private Object instantiate(Class<?> type)
      Constructs a controller class via reflection, picking a constructor that takes (NetworkService, Session, Navigator) if available, otherwise the (NetworkService, Session) or no-arg form. This keeps screen controllers declarative — they just declare the constructor they want.
      Parameters:
      type - the controller class to instantiate
      Returns:
      the constructed controller instance

    • buildNavButton

      public static Button buildNavButton(String icon, String label)
      Builds a sidebar nav button styled by client.css, with the icon and label laid out side by side.
      Parameters:
      icon - glyph shown at the start of the button
      label - text shown next to the icon
      Returns:
      a styled, unbound nav Button

    • contentNode

      public Node contentNode()
      Returns the content pane this navigator swaps screens into.
      Returns:
      the content pane this navigator swaps screens into
